title: Upstart Q4 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Upstart reported Q4 2023 revenue of $140 million, up 4% sequentially, and achieved its third consecutive quarter of positive adjusted EBITDA at $1 million. Full-year 2023 net revenue reached $514 million, down 39% from 2022. Loan volume reached 129,000 loans in Q4, up 12% sequentially, with contribution margin of 63% in Q4, up 10 percentage points year-over-year. Automation rate reached 89% of unsecured loans approved through fully automated process, with automated approvals converting at 92% versus 27% for manual processes. CEO Dave Girouard highlighted six priority areas: macro management through release of Upstart Macro Index and parallel timing curve calibration tools, automation through record 89% instant fully automated loan approvals, core strengthening through developing collateral options for personal loans and enhanced servicing capabilities, capital innovation through closing $300 million loan sale to Ares and signing $500 million origination agreement, product diversification with HELOC crossing $5 million cumulative originations across 11 states, and AI leadership with small dollar product tripling approval rates since August. Management provided Q1 2024 guidance of total revenue of approximately $125 million and adjusted EBITDA of approximately negative $25 million. The company cited rising default rates among higher FICO, higher prime borrower segments as it tightened underwriting standards.
